touches.ai_summary: Inbound request from Theo Martinez, Head of RevOps at PrimeEngine. Expressed interest in call recording and coaching solution. Mentioned currently evaluating Fireflies but looking for alternatives with better automation capabilities.

Qualification call with Theo. PrimeEngine is a 95-person eCommerce company looking to improve sales coaching and call quality. Currently manual process, no existing solution. Theo owns the evaluation but mentioned needing to align with leadership eventually. Budget discussion initiated around 7 seats.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for a call recording solution now?
questions_asked.answer: Sales team growing fast, coaching is inconsistent, managers spending too much time listening to calls manually

questions_asked.question: How does your unlimited automation tier compare to Fireflies' pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited plan offers more flexible automation rules and integrates deeper with CRM workflows

next_steps: Theo to map out exact use cases and automation needs
Schedule discovery call to review requirements in detail

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent to Theo with resources on automation capabilities and coaching workflows. Included case study from similar-sized eCommerce company. Theo responded same day confirming he reviewed materials and is ready to discuss implementation details.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform auto-tag calls by deal stage and trigger coaching alerts?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully configurable based on CRM stage and custom keywords

next_steps: Confirm discovery call for detailed walkthrough

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Theo covering detailed requirements. Focused on 7 seats for sales managers and directors. Strong interest in automation features to reduce manual QA work. Theo shared that Fireflies is in consideration but lacks some automation depth they need. No mention of exec involvement or broader stakeholder alignment.

questions_asked.question: Who else will be involved in the final decision?
questions_asked.answer: I'm driving this, will loop in our VP Sales when we're closer to a decision

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline look like?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 1-2 weeks for setup, training can run parallel

questions_asked.question: Have you allocated budget for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: We have budget approved for tools in this range, just need to finalize the vendor

objections: Timeline is tight, need to be live within 3 weeks
next_steps: Rep to prepare proposal with pricing for 7 seats
Theo to confirm internal timeline with leadership

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Theo reviewing pricing and contract terms. Presented $4,800 ACV for 7 seats on unlimited automation tier. Theo pushed back on pricing, mentioned Fireflies quote came in lower. Agreed to 37.9% discount to match competitive pressure and close within quarter. No exec sponsor joined the call. Theo confirmed he can move forward with signature authority.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Fireflies' pricing to make this an easier decision?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer a significant discount to get you started, bringing it to $4,800 annually

questions_asked.question: When would you be ready to move forward if pricing works?
questions_asked.answer: I can get this signed by end of next week if we agree on terms

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on contract length or payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: We can do annual payment or quarterly, annual gets you the best rate

objections: Initial pricing is above what Fireflies quoted
Need to move fast, any delays could impact decision
next_steps: Send updated proposal with discounted pricing
Theo to review with VP Sales informally
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with revised proposal at $4,800 ACV. Included contract draft and implementation timeline. Theo acknowledged receipt but mentioned he's still coordinating internally and will have an update in a few days. Tone was positive but less urgent than previous exchanges.
next_steps: Theo to confirm internally and respond by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email sent to Theo following up on proposal status. Theo responded that he's working through final alignment with leadership and expects to have a decision early next week. Requested patience as they finalize budget allocation. No red flags raised but timeline slipping slightly.

questions_asked.question: Can you hold the pricing for another week while we finalize?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can hold this through end of month

next_steps: Theo to provide update by Monday
Rep to check in if no response by Tuesday
